name: Earthjustice
mission: Stay informed on how we hold accountable those who break our environmental laws.
theoryOfChange: Earthjustice has a published "Commitment To Justice" page that details a "justice framework" developed by their Community, Justice, and Impact (CJI) team. This framework outlines a clear vision for advancing justice in their interactions, choices, and work, aiming to achieve a truly democratic multi-racial society that incorporates and serves all people and the planet. It emphasizes critical inquiry as a key tool to interrogate choices and understand the organization's impact. The framework guides every staff member to engage in a practice of justice that is informed, builds interconnectedness, encourages inclusive innovation, and maintains values-based accountability.

scoreSummary: Earthjustice shows strong impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Annually recognized by the nation’s chief watchdog groups for charitable organizations for transparency and efficiency

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 99% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

components.evidence: Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.evidence: Niche cause: UNKNOWN (+4); Serves underserved populations (+3)
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 27/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: Evaluation source domain 'charitywatch.org' is not a known trusted evaluator

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: High domestic burn: 99% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

risks.description: High domestic burn: 99% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

score_details.score_summary: Earthjustice shows strong impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://earthjusticehelp.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/13119586070029-Can-I-make-a-donation-to-Earthjustice-through-my-Donor-Advised-Fund)

all_citations.claim: Earthjustice operates programs like The Wild and Healthy Communities and focuses on environmental advocacy.
The organization reported total revenue of $125,960,142 and a program expense ratio of 80.2%.
Donations to Earthjustice support legal action for the environment and are eligible for general charitable giving (sadaqah).

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Earthjustice earns a 61/100 reflecting its high financial efficiency and capacity-building impact, though its specialized advocacy focus may not align with all donor priorities.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary due to the nature of complex, multi-year legal advocacy
Lower alignment for donors seeking direct humanitarian or community-specific services

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is moderate as the organization focuses on broad advocacy and systemic change rather than direct service [1]. While it is a sadaqah-eligible option for those prioritizing environmental justice, the donor fit for traditional charitable giving is considered low due to its specialized legal mission [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high impact through capacity building and legal precedent, allocating 80.2% of its expenses directly to its programs [2]. While the cost per beneficiary is high at $118,249.374, this reflects the intensive resources required for high-level environmental litigation and policy reform [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Earthjustice is a premier nonprofit public interest law organization dedicated to wielding the power of law to protect the environment and public health.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 80.2% ensuring most funds reach legal and advocacy work
Exceptional fundraising efficiency at only $0.12 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 19.3 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: Founded as a legal advocate for the natural world, Earthjustice operates major programs including The Wild and Healthy Communities to defend environmental protections [1]. The organization reported a total revenue of $125,960,142 and maintains a robust financial position to support its long-term litigation efforts [2].

all_citations.claim: premier nonprofit public interest environmental law organization
all_citations.quote: Earthjustice is the premier nonprofit public interest environmental law organization.

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Earthjustice earns a 61/100 reflecting its high financial efficiency and capacity-building impact, though its specialized advocacy focus may not align with all donor priorities.

areas_for_improvement.detail: Due to the nature of high-level legal advocacy, the <cite id="10">cost per beneficiary is estimated at $118,249</cite>, which may not appeal to donors seeking high-volume direct service impact.
Despite some international work, <cite id="11">80.2% of expenses are concentrated within the US</cite>, limiting its impact in the global south where environmental crises are often most acute.

bbb_assessment.summary: Maintains high governance standards with a large, independent board and audited financials.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization is slowly expanding its international footprint in South Africa and South America.
risk_factors: <cite id="10">High cost per beneficiary</cite> inherent to legal work
case_against.summary: The primary concern for donors is the <cite id="11">high domestic burn rate</cite> and the indirect nature of legal advocacy.

confidence.notes: Financial data is highly consistent across sources.

alignment.explanation: While the cause is urgent, the <cite id="16">alignment score of 27/50</cite> reflects that this is an advocacy-based model rather than a direct humanitarian service.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment for Muslim donors by <cite id="17">clearly stating the sadaqah-eligibility</cite> of its environmental justice programs.

credibility.explanation: Earthjustice is highly credible, holding a <cite id="12">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and maintaining <cite id="13">100% independent board members</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, the organization could pursue a full rating from Charity Navigator to provide third-party validated accountability scores.

impact.explanation: Impact is demonstrated through systemic change, such as <cite id="14">analyzing data center tax incentives across 8 states</cite> and reaching <cite id="15">100 million views</cite> for environmental education.
impact.improvement: If reserves remain significantly above policy targets, the organization should <cite id="7">document specific triggers for planned deployment</cite> of these funds.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade A - Verified outcomes and strong theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Supports partner organizations and foundations to amplify environmental legal impact.

rich_narrative.headline: Earthjustice is a premier nonprofit public interest law organization dedicated to wielding the power of law to protect the environment and public health.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who believe in systemic change through the legal system and want to fund high-stakes litigation to protect the planet.
donor_motivations: Value the power of law to hold corporations and governments accountable
Prioritize long-term environmental protections over immediate relief
Want to support tribal sovereignty and healthy communities
Prefer established institutions with a 50-year track record
giving_considerations: Donations support legal fees and expert testimony rather than direct aid
Litigation can take years to yield measurable results
Focus is primarily on U.S. policy and law
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for immediate humanitarian interventions like food or medical aid.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has verified outcomes, a strong theory of change, and tracks metrics such as curriculum usage and platform reach.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Earthjustice uses the law to preserve wild lands, build biodiversity, and protect oceans from the impacts of climate change.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: In legal advocacy, evidence of success is found in court victories and policy shifts that protect millions of people.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The scale of environmental litigation against global entities requires massive, sustained capital.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="1">exclusive focus on legal litigation</cite> rather than general public awareness or land conservation.

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="5">$0.12 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, significantly outperforming many large-scale advocacy nonprofits.
Earthjustice maintains <cite id="7">19.3 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring the ability to sustain complex, multi-year litigation regardless of short-term funding fluctuations.
With a <cite id="4">80.2% program expense ratio</cite>, the charity ensures that the vast majority of its $125M+ revenue directly funds environmental legal services.

strengths_deep_dive: Earthjustice's primary strength lies in its institutional maturity and specialized focus. Having been <cite id="1">founded in 1971</cite>, it possesses over five decades of experience in environmental law. This expertise is backed by a <cite id="8">board of 33 members</cite> providing diverse oversight. Their financial health is exceptionally strong, with <cite id="9">total assets reaching $294,773,000</cite> in 2023, which allows them to take on the world's largest polluters without fear of being out-resourced in court.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 1971, Earthjustice has established itself as the <cite id="1">premier nonprofit public interest environmental law organization</cite> in the United States. The organization operates on the principle that the law is a powerful tool for environmental protection, managing a diverse portfolio that includes <cite id="2">Climate and Energy, Healthy Communities, and Tribal Sovereignty Support</cite>. In the 2023 tax year, Earthjustice reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $125,960,142</cite>, a figure that supports its extensive litigation and advocacy efforts. The organization maintains a highly efficient financial model, dedicating a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 80.2%</cite> to its core mission of legal defense and environmental advocacy. This fiscal discipline is further evidenced by a <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.12 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions are directed toward substantive legal work rather than administrative overhead. Beyond its domestic focus, Earthjustice has expanded its reach to include <cite id="6">South Africa and South America</cite>, though the bulk of its operations remains centered on U.S. federal and state environmental protections. The organization's long-term stability is underscored by its <cite id="7">19.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for multi-year legal battles that often face political or procedural delays.
